what_it_does: This file implements a small global subscription store for channel data. It fetches /api/channels, normalizes the response into ChannelRow objects, and exposes the resulting channels list plus loading, error, and open flags through a React hook that subscribes to store updates.
observations: Uses a mutable global state object and a Set of subscriber callbacks for re-reactivity.
loadChannels increments a loadSeq counter to drop stale responses.
The fetch response is normalized by falling back through Array.isArray(d), d.channels, d.items, and finally [].
ChannelRow keeps members as Record<string, unknown> even though the comment notes the API serves an object that DNA expects as an array.
ensureStarted is declared but is an empty no-op.
useChannels registers a callback that forces a re-render and returns the current global state.
No automatic fetch happens on mount; openChannels triggers the load.
standouts: Heavy conceptual framing in comments (FACE-1, fabric, DNA, registry-is-truth, fail-loud).
Explicit admission that the API shape (members object) differs from the downstream adapter expectation (members array).
conventions: Global store with manual subscription pattern.
React useEffect + force-update for external state synchronization.
Async fetch with try/catch and user-facing error message.
TypeScript type aliases for API-shaped records and store state.
concerns: ensureStarted is a stub and does nothing.
useChannels returns the live global state reference, which is fine but relies on the subscription callback to trigger React re-renders.
Error handling swallows the original exception and replaces it with a generic message.
Response normalization could silently produce an empty channels list if the payload lacks channels/items and is not an array.
Members typed as Record<string, unknown> loses the documented {participation, added} structure.
notes: A thin, self-contained channel data store with a lot of surrounding design vocabulary; the actual code is a fetch-and-normalize subscription store.
questions: What is ensureStarted intended to initialize?
How exactly does the host normalize members from an object to an array for DNA?
What triggers openChannels in the UI?
Are there pagination or refresh intervals for /api/channels?
Why are status, mode, purpose, origin, created, seq, and last_activity omitted from ChannelRow despite being described in the data shape?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose largely matches the code: it loads /api/channels, holds raw rows, and fails loudly on error. The mismatch is that ensureStarted is only a stub and the store does not auto-start; it waits for openChannels.
apparent_themes: Boundary normalization: keep the store dumb, push rendering elsewhere.
intention_signals: It is trying to be a minimal, honest data layer for a graph-oriented channel surface, deferring all rendering and graph layout to an external DNA/render system while keeping the local store focused on fetch and state holding.
novelty: The file mixes a very simple fetch store with an elaborate design narrative (FACE-1 breadth surface, live fabric graph, DNA organisms) and explicitly documents a known API-to-adapter shape mismatch.
contribution: A compact, race-guarded channel fetch store with subscriber-based React integration and clear normalization of an /api/channels payload; its local behavior and error-handling policy are concrete pieces that a fused system could retain.
summary_for_embedding: A React subscription store that fetches and normalizes /api/channels into typed ChannelRow records, exposing loading, error, open state, and a useChannels hook for a channel-graph surface.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a dumb, reactive data layer for a channel graph view, keeping rendering concerns external and surfacing honest load failures.

symbols.does: Type alias describing a normalized channel record returned from /api/channels.
symbols.description: Defines the store's channel shape with id, kind, name, posts, coordinator, promoted_to, last_activity, and a loosely typed members object; it intentionally keeps only the fields the store currently consumes.

symbols.does: Type alias for the store's runtime state.
symbols.description: Describes the state object held by the store: the channels list, loading and error flags, and an open flag that indicates whether the channel surface is being viewed.

symbols.does: Patches the global state and notifies all subscribers.
symbols.description: Creates a new state object from the current state and a partial patch, then iterates the subscriber set so React hooks and other listeners re-render.

symbols.does: Fetches /api/channels and normalizes the response into ChannelRow objects.
symbols.description: Race-guarded async loader that sets loading state, parses the JSON, falls back through common array wrappers, maps each raw record into a ChannelRow, and either updates channels or sets a user-facing error message.

symbols.does: Marks the channel surface as open and refreshes the channel data.
symbols.description: Sets the open flag to true and immediately calls loadChannels so the user sees the latest fabric when opening the view.

symbols.does: Marks the channel surface as closed.
symbols.description: Sets the open flag to false; does not clear channels or cancel an in-flight request.
Checks a started boolean and returns immediately; it reserves a lifecycle hook but currently performs no setup.

symbols.does: React hook that subscribes a component to the global channel store.
symbols.description: Registers a callback that increments a local state counter to force re-renders, ensures the store is 'started', and returns the current global state on every render.
A closure passed to the subscription set that triggers a React force-update whenever the store calls its subscribers.
